other_observers: Bob Spencer; Unknown, but I wasn't the one who found it.
specific_location: Prince Lake #2 or Hiram Prince Reservior in some maps
description: At first it didn't appear that there were any ducks on this lake where a Eurasian wigeon had been reported. There were, however, a few Amerian wigeon on the seepage pond below the dam. I was just about ready to go back and check them over again when I spotted a few green-winged teal and American wigeon up on the muddy shore across the lake. They all had their heads down feeding. I spotted the Eurasian wigeon intermingled with the 30 American wigeon feeding on the shore. It's gold crown was readily apparent among the female and male American wigeons. Structurally, he was like the American wigeons, but has a russet-colored head and gold crown. His sides were also grayer as was his back. Even at a couple of hundred yards the male EUWI stuck out like a sore thumb. I observed the bird on 3 seperate occasions today. Everytime it was feeding on the shore and I never saw it near the water. At one of my stops here, I ran into Bob Spencer.Behaviors: The bird was feeding on the muddy shoreline with American wigeons.Call: not observedPlumage: breeding/alternate
